task_context.actionable_question: Does one student face more structural disadvantage than the other?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Identify background-driven performance differences. Avoid causal claims.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: The comparison of the two students reveals significant differences in their educational backgrounds, particularly in parental education levels and associated disadvantage scores. Student 1 has a higher parental education level and lower disadvantage score compared to Student 2.

Insights: Parental Education Level: Student 1 has a higher parental education level compared to Student 2, which may indicate a more supportive academic environment. | Disadvantage Score: Student 1 has a lower disadvantage score than Student 2, suggesting that Student 1 faces fewer structural disadvantages.

Educational implications: Students with higher parental education levels may have better access to resources and support, impacting their academic performance.

Recommendations: high - Provide additional support to Student 2 - Given the higher disadvantage score, Student 2 may benefit from targeted academic support to mitigate structural disadvantages.

explanation.summary: The comparison of the two students reveals significant differences in their educational backgrounds, particularly in parental education levels and associated disadvantage scores. Student 1 has a higher parental education level and lower disadvantage score compared to Student 2.

insights.title: Parental Education Level
insights.description: Student 1 has a higher parental education level compared to Student 2, which may indicate a more supportive academic environment.

evidence.context: Student 1's mother has a higher education level than Student 2's mother.
Student 1's father has a higher education level than Student 2's father.

insights.title: Disadvantage Score
insights.description: Student 1 has a lower disadvantage score than Student 2, suggesting that Student 1 faces fewer structural disadvantages.

evidence.context: Student 1's disadvantage score is significantly lower than Student 2's.

educational_implications: Students with higher parental education levels may have better access to resources and support, impacting their academic performance.

recommendations.rationale: Given the higher disadvantage score, Student 2 may benefit from targeted academic support to mitigate structural disadvantages.

confidence.reason: The data is complete and directly comparable between the two students.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
